methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Stratolaunch is a private-stage space and aerospace company founded by Paul Allen and Burt Rutan, currently owned by Cerberus. The valuation landscape includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, though the immediate peer set at identical stage and scale is sparse.

Two standard triangulation methods proved inapplicable here. Peer-median anchoring requires a minimum of three same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tier; only two same-stage private peers were identified, falling short of the threshold needed to support a reliable median. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be executed without disclosed revenue: the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S is 59.8x across the 6 public tickers, but Stratolaunch's revenue is not on file, precluding multiplication to an implied valuation.

The broader sector universe—23 same-sector peers—offers directional context but insufficient granularity for quantitative synthesis. Without either a dense peer median or auditable revenue figures, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range. Meaningful triangulation would require either (1) disclosure of Stratolaunch's revenue or similar financial metrics, or (2) identification of additional same-stage private comparables with published valuations. Until those inputs are available, valuation rests on qualitative factors and transaction history rather than market-based methodology.
